title: Are Elder Millennial Women in America Ok? A Historical Accounting
description: To be human is to go through multiple cycles of belief and cynicism throughout your lifetime. To quote the canonic meme guy sitting at a table, debate me. But I think that people my age have grown up against a
hook: Elder millennial women raised on Girl Power are now questioning everything—a reckoning with what we were promised versus what actually happened.

title: Book Review: ‘The Housewives Underground,’ by Kaitlyn Tiffany
description: In “The Housewives Underground,” the Atlantic writer Kaitlyn Tiffany salutes a loose network of skeptics who questioned the findings of the Warren Report.
hook: A network of housewives questioned the Warren Report before the internet existed—Kaitlyn Tiffany on obsessive women doing their own detective work.

title: ‘Toy Story 5’ Shows Hollywood Is Still Not Releasing Enough Kids Movies
description: The staggering $312 million global opening for the fifth entry in the Pixar animated franchise is the latest proof that family films are undefeated at the box office.
hook: Toy Story 5 made $312 million because studios stopped making children's movies—what that tells us about risk and audience hunger.

title: Book Review: “Nebraska,” by Monica Datta
description: Monica Datta’s “Nebraska” is a maximalist, continent-spanning story of a mother killing her youngest child, as relayed by a highly idiosyncratic psychoanalyst.
hook: A psychoanalyst recounts a mother murdering her child across a continent in maximalist detail—Monica Datta's Nebraska is as unsettling as it sounds.

title: Black Swan at the A.R.T: an unearthly medley of film, musical theatre and ballet
description: Boston's ambitious adaptation leverages the tricks of various performing arts trades and, after all these years, finally sets Natalie Portman’s doomed ballerina free.
hook: This adaptation finally frees Natalie Portman's ballerina from the screen by collapsing film, theatre, and dance into something genuinely unhinged.
